Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

This can be calculated in userspace from the "total" and "free" amounts, no need to add extra /proc files for this.  As an added benefit, these values exist for all versions of Lustre, so no worries about compatibility with old versions that don't have the "used" amounts.

Patch Set 1:

Hi Andreas,

I know it is a question whether it is really necessary to add used capacity/inode, but according to the requirement of our customers, it is.

The used capacity/inode can be calculated indeed from total - free. However, mathmatical operation is not supported in all monitoring solutions, at least not in the framework that we are currelty using. We  adding the support, but still, I think other users would still find that they can not get the used capacity/inode easily. And calculating it manually from total and free capacity/inode obviously is not a good experience for administrators.

Patch Set 1:

> The used capacity/inode can be calculated indeed from total - free.
 > However, mathematical operation is not supported in all monitoring
 > solutions, at least not in the framework that we are currently
 > using. We  adding the support, but still, I think other users would
 > still find that they can not get the used capacity/inode easily.
 > And calculating it manually from total and free capacity/inode
 > obviously is not a good experience for administrators.

Since this feature wouldn't be available until at least 2.12 it also doesn't help your customers, while (total - free) is available at all of your sites today.

Patch Set 1:

We included this patch in our branch for long a while(more than a year) and it's running our monitoring against based filesused stats at many customer sites. we know total - free makes filesused, but still a lot of people are interseted in checking filesused quickloy.

Patch Set 1:

Any particular reason why doing statfs on the underlying mountpoint dos not work for you? It's already there and does not depend on any unlanded patches and the like?

 # df -h
 /dev/mapper/mds1_flakey                                                                              123M  1.9M  110M   2% /mnt/lustre-mds1
 /dev/mapper/ost1_flakey                                                                              318M   14M  279M   5% /mnt/lustre-ost1
 /dev/mapper/ost2_flakey                                                                              318M   14M  279M   5% /mnt/lustre-ost2
 192.168.10.226@tcp:/lustre                                                                           636M   27M  557M   5% /mnt/lustre

Patch Set 1:

Using "df" to collect the information means that 1) We need to use another plugin to get the information, not the same plugin that collects information from /proc/fs/lustre 2) the new plugin need to filter information of other file systems.

Abandoned

abandon the patch, since we have math support in collectd and do not need it

commit.subject: LU-10873 lustre: Add support for used inode/space on MDT/OST proc
commit.message:
LU-10873 lustre: Add support for used inode/space on MDT/OST proc

We have a monitoring system which can get the information from
the /proc entries of Lustre. However, the Lustre doesn't export the
used inode/space on MDT/OST, this patch adds used inode/space info
in ofd/lod layer:

/proc/fs/lustre/osd-*/testfs-OST0000/filesused
/proc/fs/lustre/lod/testfs-MDT0000-mdtlov/fileused
/proc/fs/lustre/osd-*/testfs-OST0000/kbytesused
/proc/fs/lustre/lod/testfs-MDT0000-mdtlov/kbytesused

originally-from: Developer Zheng Xiang
Signed-off-by: Wang Shilong <wshilong@ddn.com>
Signed-off-by: Qian Yingjin <qian@ddn.com>
Change-Id: Id747ce95cce922bb5f402a9a910e42f0d3880f6a
